title: Uruguayan Beef Quesadilla con Ensalada de Milenrama
description: A tangy and crispy Uruguayan-inspired quesadilla filled with fajita-seasoned beef and topped with a refreshing milenrama salad.

ingredients.name: Tender steak (Uruguayan ribeye or flank steak)

instructions: Begin by marinating the beef strips in a mixture of lime juice, garlic, and fajita seasoning for at least 30 minutes.
Heat a large skillet over medium-high heat. Remove the beef from the marinade, letting any excess liquid drip off.
Add the beef to the skillet and cook for 3-4 minutes per side, or until browned and cooked through.
Transfer the beef to a plate and cover with foil to keep warm.
In the same skillet, brush the tortilla with olive oil and place it in the skillet for 30 seconds to 1 minute, until slightly crispy and pliable.
Sprinkle shredded cheese on half of the tortilla, followed by a few strips of the cooked beef.
Fold the tortilla in half to enclose the filling.
Cook for 2-3 minutes or until the tortilla is crispy and the cheese is melted.
Flip the quesadilla and cook for an additional 2-3 minutes, until the other side is also crispy.
Transfer the quesadilla to a plate and top with a spoonful of the milenrama salad.
Garnish with chopped cilantro and serve immediately.
For the milenrama salad, combine chopped milenrama, a squeeze of lime juice, and a sprinkle of cilantro in a bowl. Toss to combine.
